REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT JOSHUA SLOAN DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT SUGGEST ANY AMENDMENTS THAT WOULD ADDRESS THE ISSUES FORMING THE BASIS OF THIS COURT'S DECISION ON CO-CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER HEARD ON JANUARY 5, 2024. AS OF THE TIME OF THE FILING OF THE LATE OPPOSITION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAD THE BENEFIT OF THE COURT'S RULING FOR OVER 2 MONTHS, HENCE, HAD AMPLE TIME TO PROVIDE PROPOSED AMENDMENT SUGGESTIONS FOR THE COURT TO CONSIDER IN RULING ON THIS DEMURRER. REPLY BY CROSS-DEFENDANT PALLADIAN WEST, INC. TO CROSS-COMPLAINANTS OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100222988) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PALLADIAN WEST, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION FKA SLOAN REALTY GROUP, INC.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS-DEFENDANT PALLADIAN WEST, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE VALIDITY OF THE AGENT'S CLAIM WAS RESOLVED BY A FINAL ORDER OF THE BANKRUPTCY COURT THAT HAD JURISDICTION OVER THE CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF THE COMMON NUCLEUS OF FACTS PERTAINING TO THE LISTING AGREEMENT AND ENTITLEMENT TO THE FEES UNDER THE AGREEMENT. THERE IS NOTHING ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT TO SUGGEST THAT THE SELLER'S CLAIMS WERE NOT DISCLOSED IN BANKRUPTCY DUE TO INADVERTENCE OR MISTAKE OR COULD NOT HAVE BEEN PROPERLY PRESENTED TO THE BANKRUPTCY COURT VIA AN OBJECTION. SEE HAMILTON V. GREENWICH INVESTORS (2011) 195 CAL.APP.4TH 1602; AH QUIN V. COUNTY OF KAUAI DEPT. OF TRANSP. (9TH CIR. 2013) 733 F.3D 267; IN RE BEVAN (2003) 327 F.3D 994; STERN V. MARSHALL (2011) 564 U.S. 462; AND IN RE MCELWEE (2012) 469 B.R. 566, CROSS-COMPLAINANT DOES NOT SUGGEST THAT ANY AMENDMENTS MAY BE MADE IN GOOD FAITH TO OVERCOME THE ISSUE RAISED BY THE DEMURRER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT JOSHUA SLOAN DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Cross-Complaint does not suggest any amendments that would address the issues forming the basis of this Court's decision on co-cross-defendant's demurrer heard on January 5, 2024. As of the time of the filing of the late opposition, Cross-Complainant had the benefit of the Court's ruling for over 2 months, hence, had ample time to provide proposed amendment suggestions for the Court to consider in ruling on this demurrer.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Continued To Jul-03-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Cheryl C. Rouse; Law Offices of Rouse & Bahlert; 415-575-9444; appearing as counsel for plaintiff and cross-defendant Palladian West, Inc. fka Sloan Realty Group, Inc.
Luke Jackson; Vokshori Law Group, APLC; 213-986-4323; appearing as counsel for defendant and cross-complainant Esperanza Ahumada.

Mr. Jackson contests the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT PALLADIAN WEST, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The validity of the agent's claim was resolved by a final order of the bankruptcy court that had jurisdiction over the claims arising out of the common nucleus of facts pertaining to the listing agreement and entitlement to the fees under the agreement. There is nothing on the face of the complaint to suggest that the seller's claims were not disclosed in bankruptcy due to inadvertence or mistake or could not have been properly presented to the bankruptcy court via an objection. See Hamilton v. Greenwich Investors (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1602; Ah Quin v. County of Kauai Dept. of Transp. (9th Cir. 2013) 733 F.3d 267; In re Bevan (2003) 327 F.3d 994; Stern v. Marshall (2011) 564 U.S. 462; and In Re McElwee (2012) 469 B.R. 566, Cross-complainant does not suggest that any amendments may be made in good faith to overcome the issue raised by the demurrer.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
